_note: Anchor canon — public-domain doctrinal & primary law. Highest reasoning-density per GB.

sources.name: Constitute Project (200+ constitutions, structured)
sources.coverage: Every world constitution, machine-tagged by topic; covers OmniLegal core jurisdictions and 180+ others.
sources.access: Open JSON API at constituteproject.org/service/; no key required
sources.license: Constitute Project terms — academic re-use, attribution required

sources.name: Doctrinal Canon (Internet Archive public domain)
sources.coverage: Blackstone, Coke, Story, Pollock & Maitland, Maine, Bentham, Austin, Salmond, Holdsworth, Grotius, Vattel, Oppenheim, Justinian Institutes/Digest, Wigmore, Cooley, Federalist Papers.

sources.name: OHCHR JURIS (UN Treaty Body Jurisprudence)
sources.coverage: Decisions from UN Treaty Bodies (HRC, CESCR, CERD, CEDAW, CAT, CRC, CRPD, CMW) — irreplaceable for human-rights reasoning.

sources.name: ICRC IHL Customary Rules + Treaty Database
sources.coverage: Customary IHL rules, Geneva Conventions + Additional Protocols, national IHL implementations.
sources.access: Public; ICRC permission flag in env (ICRC_PERMISSION_CONFIRMED)
sources.license: ICRC open access (academic). Set ICRC_PERMISSION_CONFIRMED=1 once you read their ToS.

sources.name: UNCITRAL CLOUT (Case Law on UNCITRAL Texts)
sources.coverage: National court decisions applying CISG, UNCITRAL Model Laws on arbitration / e-commerce / insolvency / secured transactions.

sources.name: Refworld (UNHCR Global Law and Policy Database)
sources.coverage: Country-of-origin information, asylum jurisprudence, position papers, legal-policy guidance.

sources.name: HCCH (Hague Conference on Private International Law)
sources.coverage: All Hague Conventions full text + status tables (apostille, child abduction, choice of court, etc.).

sources.name: Italaw (investment-treaty arbitration awards)
sources.coverage: ICSID, UNCITRAL, ICC, PCA, SCC investor-state awards — core source for investment law.

sources.name: UN Treaty Collection (UNTS)
sources.coverage: All multilateral and bilateral treaties deposited with the UN Secretary-General; status, parties, reservations.

sources.name: Comparative Constitutions Project
sources.coverage: Structured features of every constitution + historical changes; complements Constitute.
